title: Penstemon schaffneri
intro: Penstemon schaffneri, also known as Schaffner's beardtongue, is a perennial plant species belonging to the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for wound healing, sore throats, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Penstemon schaffneri include iridoid glycosides, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Penstemon schaffneri include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to soothe respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Penstemon schaffneri is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae, commonly known as the Schaffner's beardtongue. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is classified as a perennial herb, with tall spikes of white or pink flowers. Penstemon schaffneri is also known to be a member of the genus Penstemon, which comprises over 250 species of flowering plants. The classification of Penstemon schaffneri is further supported by its distinctive characteristics, such as its hairy leaves and stems.
